title: Not Just Oil: 3 Fertilizer Stocks Boosted by Hormuz Closure
author: Dan Schmidt
description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has disrupted approximately 30-35% of global fertilizer supply, including nitrogen, phosphate, and potash. This supply shock is benefiting North American fertilizer producers, particularly those with domestic natural gas advantages. Three stocks are positioned to gain from higher fertilizer prices amid the extended shortage.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Diversified fertilizer producer with 20% potash market control and 1,500+ North American locations. Upgraded to Buy by Wells Fargo and Jeffries with price targets of $100 and $96. Up 25% YTD with strong uptrend momentum, positioned to capture margin gains across all three nutrient categories.

Pure-play nitrogen producer with structural advantage from affordable U.S. natural gas. Can offer competitive prices globally while expanding margins due to low input costs versus European and Asian competitors. Up 60% YTD with strong bullish technical momentum and sustained nitrogen price catalysts.

Riskier profile due to dependence on sulfur transported through Strait of Hormuz, limiting margin expansion potential. Recent Q4 2025 EPS miss caused 5% stock decline. Up only 18% YTD, lagging peers. Technical consolidation pattern suggests potential recovery, but operational hurdles present headwinds.

title: Iran War Ripples Across Global Commodities, BMO Warns
author: Stjepan Kalinic
description: Escalating Middle East conflict is disrupting global commodity markets, with oil, fertilizers, and chemicals experiencing significant supply pressures. BMO analysts warn that crude prices remain undervalued relative to supply risks, while fertilizer and chemical producers face margin expansion opportunities. Battery production and nickel markets face indirect threats through sulfur supply disruptions.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Nitrogen fertilizer producer benefits from 30% price increase and strengthened North American competitiveness due to European gas cost disadvantages and Middle Eastern production disruptions
Fertilizer producer positioned to benefit from tighter supply outlook and higher margins as Middle Eastern production faces disruption
Petrochemical producer expected to see margin expansion from polyethylene and polypropylene pricing increases due to Middle East supply constraints
Petrochemical producer benefits from tightening polyethylene and polypropylene markets with potential utilization rates approaching full capacity
Petrochemical producer positioned to benefit from margin expansion in plastics markets due to Middle East supply disruptions
Titanium dioxide producer benefits from sulfur shortages supporting pricing in the titanium dioxide market
Titanium dioxide producer positioned to benefit from sulfur shortage-driven pricing support in titanium dioxide markets

title: Society Pass Incorporated (Nasdaq: SOPA) Rolls Out M&A Strategy to Capture Potential Significant Valuation Opportunities in Global Landscape of Privately Held Companies
author: Raynauld Liang
description: Society Pass Inc. plans to execute a comprehensive mergers and acquisition strategy targeting privately-held companies in Southeast Asia, Europe, and North America, focusing on AI data centres, travel, digital advertising, and telecommunications sectors.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company demonstrates successful track record of company incubation, strategic growth, and potential value generation through targeted acquisitions
Successfully listed on Nasdaq, growing from under $5 million acquisition to over $170 million market capitalization

title: Acceleware Ltd. Reports Third Quarter 2025 Financial and Operating Results
author: Geoff Clark
description: Acceleware reported a comprehensive loss of $1.7 million for the first nine months of 2025, focusing on developing RF XL 2.0 technology for enhanced oil production and exploring critical minerals processing applications.

title: Growers Edge Funds First Loan, Projects Over $30 Million in Mortgages by Year-End
author: Jack Mchugh
description: Agricultural finance technology firm Growers Edge has partnered with a secondary market provider to fund its first mortgage loan, aiming to help finance over $30 million in agricultural mortgages by end of 2025 and provide financial services for farmers who typically struggle to meet traditional lending criteria.
